concerns.text: FR-005 (Simulate Data with Known Ground Truth) and US-4 Acceptance Scenario 1 require generating 'at least 1000 simulated datasets per effect size'. Task T031 defers this count to '[deferred: research decision]' without a task to define or enforce the minimum 1000 count. This leaves the Success Criterion SC-004 (CI half-width ±0.02) untestable as the sample size is not fixed in the implementation plan.

FR-008 requires a sensitivity analysis 'sweeping α across a range of small values' (specifically {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} per Spec Assumptions). Task T043 mentions sweeping 'low-magnitude values' but lacks a specific task to implement the loop over the defined set {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} and record the results. Without explicit iteration over these values, the requirement to assess false-positive rates across this specific range is not covered.

FR-010 requires computing SHA-256 checksums for 'all downloaded datasets'. Task T014 schedules checksumming only 'after filtering' (T016) and explicitly states it checksums the 'final usable dataset'. This creates a gap: the raw downloaded datasets (which may include those later excluded for non-normality or size) are not checksummed as required by FR-010 and Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which mandates checksumming 'all downloaded datasets' before modification/filtering.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 and FR-002 require excluding datasets with N < 30 and logging the reason. While T016 implements the filtering logic, there is no specific task to implement the 'exclusion log' or ensure the exclusion reason is recorded in a persistent log file (distinct from the general pipeline log in T009) as required by the 'logging' clause in FR-002 and the Edge Cases section.
